memberdef.argsstring: (self, Optional[str] line_type=None, Optional[int] level_1=None, Optional[int] level_2=None, Optional[Union[bool, str]] can_be_multiline=None, str default_line_type=HierarchyLevel.raw_text, Optional[int] default_level_1=None, Optional[int] default_level_2=None)
memberdef.name: __init__

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: init method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: match method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: get_hierarchy_level method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: _get_line_type method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: _get_level_1 method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: _get_level_2 method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: _get_regexp_level_2 method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: _get_can_be_multiline method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: TagPattern for document structure extraction pipeline

detaileddescription.para: Pattern for using information from readers saved in line.metadata.tag_hierarchy_level. Can be useful for paragraph extraction in PDF documents and images, because PDF and image readers save information about paragraphs in line.metadata.tag_hierarchy_level.can_be_multiline.
.. seealso:: Please see :ref:`readers_line_types` if you need information, which line types can be extracted by each reader. Example of library usage:

.. code-block:: python import re from dedoc.structure_extractors import DefaultStructureExtractor from dedoc.structure_extractors.patterns import TagPattern
reader = ... structure_extractor = DefaultStructureExtractor() patterns = [TagPattern(default_line_type="raw_text")] document = reader.read(file_path=file_path) document = structure_extractor.extract(document=document, parameters={"patterns": patterns}) Example of API usage:
